title: The v1 bar: flagship 15-20 minute agent-authored episode
short_description: An agent produces a coherent 15-20 minute script-driven episode passing verify end-to-end, Arnav watches it once and approves the style, and every human-caught miss feeds the Flow-3 autopsy loop into new constraint rules until it passes.
long_description_md:
# The v1 bar: flagship 15-20 minute agent-authored episode

## Why this exists

PROJECT.md's success definition, verbatim: "an AI agent, using only the library + CLI, produces a coherent 15-20 minute script-driven silent video (maps + characters + text, subtitles optional) that passes verification end-to-end — and the founder watches it once and says 'yes, that's the style.'" Product-spec success metric 1 restates it as the v1 bar. Every prior phase (0 through 6) built a capability; every Phase 7 onboarding task proved an agent can use those capabilities cold. This task is where all of it gets pointed at the actual thing the whole project exists to produce — not a demo clip, the real bar.

This task is deliberately last. Roadmap Phase 7 intro: "Last before launch because the docs must describe the real, frozen surface." Everything upstream — map engine, both style packs, taste constraints, long-form scale, docs, cookbook, fresh-agent test — must be real and load-bearing before this runs, or a miss here can't be told apart from an unfinished dependency.

## Roadmap fit

Phase 7, final done-when bullet, verbatim: "The v1 bar: an agent produces a coherent 15-20 minute script-driven episode passing verify end-to-end, and Arnav watches it once and says 'yes, that's the style.' Misses feed the Flow-3 autopsy loop until this passes." Depends on `longform-pipeline-proof` (Phase 6 — proves a full 15-20 min silent episode renders end-to-end at all, the pipeline-correctness proof this task builds the polished flagship on top of) and `fresh-agent-test` (Phase 7 — proves an agent can work this codebase cold before being trusted with the flagship-scale task).

## Required scope

**The episode.** An agent authors a genuinely new 15-20 minute narration-script-driven project (100+ beats per the product-spec scale requirement) using maps, characters, text, and subtitles — a real topic, not a stress-test stub. `anima verify --json` on the finished project reports `pass: true`, zero error-severity findings, project duration in the 15-20 minute band.

**The render.** `anima render` produces the full-resolution, full-length MP4. Output is checked, not merely produced, per the persona's definition of done: NFR performance budget met (≤60 min render time on the reference machine for typical content, ≥8fps sustained throughput, ≤2GB memory), and output written atomically with no corruption.

**The founder checkpoint.** Arnav watches the finished render once. Pass/fail is his call — "yes, that's the style" or a named list of misses. This is genuine human judgment (persona: brutal honesty, no diplomatic softening) — not a rubber stamp, not something an automated check can stand in for.

**The Flow-3 autopsy loop.** Every miss the founder catches — a defect `verify` passed but a human would flag, per product-spec success metric 2 — gets logged, traced to a new or tightened constraint rule (or a documented reason it's out of deterministic-verification reach), a regression fixture added so the same miss can never silently reappear, and the episode re-rendered. This repeats until the founder checkpoint passes clean. The task is not done at 'render exists' — it's done when the loop actually terminates in approval, with the miss list showing zero open items, not abandoned mid-list.

## Out of scope

- Building any new engine capability, node type, style-pack asset, or verify-rule *family* wholesale — this task only tightens/extends existing rules in response to real misses; a miss needing a structurally new capability (not covered by any Phase 0-6 task) is a scope gap to flag, not silently build here.
- The fresh-agent onboarding test itself — `fresh-agent-test` proves the agent can work cold; this task is the founder-judged content bar, a different measurement.
- Launch assets, PyPI publish, Show HN/r/Python posts — Phase 8 launch scope; this task's episode may later be reused/cut for launch assets, but producing launch-ready cuts is not this task's job.
- Multiple episodes or genre breadth — one flagship episode clearing the bar is the done condition, not a content library.
roadmap_phase: Phase 7: Agent onboarding surface + the v1 bar

deliverables.description: Agent-authored 15-20 minute, 100+ beat episode project (maps + characters + text + subtitles, real topic) whose `anima verify --json` output reports pass:true, zero error-severity findings, and total project duration within the 900-1200s band.

Full-resolution, full-length render produced via `anima render`, output MP4 written atomically and uncorrupted, NFR performance budget checked on the reference machine (render time <=60min for typical content, sustained >=8fps, memory <=2GB), reusing the perf-capture harness established by longform-pipeline-proof.

Founder style checkpoint: Arnav watches the finished full-length render once end to end and either approves the style outright or names concrete misses. This is genuine human judgment, not automatable.

verification.steps: 1. Produce the current-best full-length render from the flagship project. 2. Arnav watches it once, start to finish, at normal speed. 3. Verdict is either 'yes, that's the style' (approved, logged) or a concrete, itemized list of misses (each becomes a Flow-3 autopsy entry). 4. Log the verdict and date in docs/cofounder/DECISIONS.md regardless of outcome.

deliverables.description: Flow-3 autopsy log (structured artifact, e.g. docs/cofounder/specs/flagship-autopsy-log.json) recording every founder-caught miss, each mapped to the constraint-rule code/fixture that closed it plus a regression test proving it can't silently reappear. Test asserts the log's open-miss count is zero on the version that passed the founder checkpoint — the loop actually terminated in approval.
